panels.description: Request rate through inference gateways, grouped by gateway workload. Shows traffic volume at the ingress layer before it reaches the scheduler.
panels.title: Gateway Request Rate by Gateway

panels.description: Gateway request rate broken down by HTTP response code. Shows the full traffic profile including successful (200) and error responses.
panels.title: Gateway Request Rate by Response Code

panels.description: 95th percentile request duration at the gateway (Envoy proxy), grouped by gateway workload. This is the outermost latency measurement — delta with E2E latency P99 reveals ingress overhead.
panels.title: Gateway Latency P95 by Gateway

panels.description: Kuadrant authorization gateway decisions: allowed, denied, and error rates.
panels.title: Kuadrant Auth Decisions

panels.description: Total number of LLMInferenceService resources in the cluster
panels.title: LLMInferenceServices

panels.description: Total number of LLMInferenceServiceConfig resources in the cluster
panels.title: LLMInferenceServiceConfigs

panels.description: Total number of ready serving pods across all inference pools
panels.title: Ready Pods

panels.description: Total successful request rate across all model servers
panels.title: Total Request Rate

panels.description: Percentage of non-2xx HTTP responses across all model servers. Green < 1%, yellow >= 1%, red >= 5%.
panels.title: HTTP Error Rate

panels.description: P99 end-to-end request latency across all model servers
panels.title: E2E Latency P99

panels.description: User-facing model names currently being served, with the model server (LLMInferenceService) they map to.
panels.title: Served Models

panels.description: Request rate per model (top 10). Click a series to drill down to Model Performance dashboard.
panels.title: Request Rate by Model Server

panels.description: HTTP error rate per model. Click a series to drill down to Failure & Diagnostics.
panels.title: Error Rate by Model Server

panels.description: End-to-end latency P95 per model. Identifies which models have latency issues.
panels.title: E2E Latency P95 by Model Server

panels.description: Average KV cache utilization per model. High utilization (>90%) indicates capacity risk.
panels.title: KV Cache Utilization by Model Server

panels.description: Total requests waiting in queue per model. Growing queues indicate capacity pressure.
panels.title: Requests Waiting by Model Server

panels.description: Number of ready pods per inference pool. Tracks serving capacity over time.
panels.title: Ready Pods by Pool

panels.description: Seconds since the last successful metric scrape per model. If this exceeds 60s, data may be stale. Above 300s, the monitoring pipeline is likely broken.
panels.title: Data Staleness Detector

panels.description: Aggregate token processing throughput across the cluster (input and output tokens per second)
panels.title: Token Throughput (Cluster)

panels.description: Rate of SLO violations by violation type (TTFT exceeded, TPOT exceeded). Directly measures user-facing quality degradation.
panels.title: SLO Violations by Type

panels.description: Error rate per InferenceModel from the scheduler. Captures errors at the scheduling boundary, including routing failures.
panels.title: Scheduler Error Rate by Model

panels.description: Current number of in-flight requests per InferenceModel at the scheduler. Shows real-time load distribution.
panels.title: Running Requests by Model (Scheduler)

panels.description: Pool saturation level from the flow control layer (0.0 = empty, 1.0 = fully saturated). Only available when the flow control FeatureGate is enabled.
panels.title: Pool Saturation (Flow Control)

panels.description: Request rate by InferenceModel and target model at the scheduler. Tracks demand and reveals traffic shifts.
panels.title: Scheduler Request Rate by Model

panels.description: Cluster-wide rate of failed NIXL KV cache transfers per model server. Any non-zero rate indicates RDMA/network issues in the disaggregated prefill-decode path.
panels.title: NIXL Failed Transfers by Model Server

panels.description: Cluster-wide count of engines in each sleep state. Sleeping engines save GPU memory but cause cold-start latency on wakeup.
panels.title: Engine Sleep State (Cluster-Wide)

title: AI / Model Serving / LLMs / Cluster Health Overview
